Property.title: Property
Property.description: Represents a rental property managed within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Property entity.

ownerId.description: The UID of the user who owns this property.

propertyType.description: Type of property (e.g., flat, house, bungalow, maisonette, studio, HMO).

status.description: Status of the property (e.g., vacant, occupied, under maintenance).

bedrooms.description: Number of bedrooms in the property.

bathrooms.description: Number of bathrooms in the property.

Tenant.title: Tenant
Tenant.description: Represents a tenant renting a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tenant entity.

ownerId.description: The UID of the user who owns the property associated with this tenant.

notes.description: Additional notes about the tenant or tenancy.

MaintenanceLog.title: MaintenanceLog
MaintenanceLog.description: Represents a maintenance log entry for a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MaintenanceLog entity.

urgency.description: Urgency level of the maintenance issue.

dateLogged.description: Date when the maintenance issue was logged.

status.description: The current status of the maintenance task (e.g., Open, In Progress, Complete).

Inspection.title: Inspection
Inspection.description: Represents a scheduled inspection for a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inspection entity.

scheduledDate.description: Date and time the inspection is scheduled for.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a document associated with a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

ownerId.description: The UID of the user who owns this document.

documentType.description: Type of the document (e.g. Tenancy Agreement, EPC).

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Represents an expense related to a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

ownerId.description: The UID of the user who owns this expense log.

expenseType.description: Category of the expense (e.g., Repairs, Utilities).

paidBy.description: Who paid for the expense (e.g., Landlord, Tenant).

definition.description: Collection to store user-related data.  Security rules will enforce that only the authenticated user can access their own document. This path contains user profile information.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Collection to store rental property information. This collection contains properties that are managed within the application.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the property.

definition.description: Subcollection to store maintenance logs for each property. This provides a clear hierarchy for managing maintenance records related to a specific property.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the property.

params.name: maintenanceLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for the maintenance log entry.

definition.description: Subcollection to store inspection schedules for each property.  Maintains inspection records specific to a property, aiding in organized tracking.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the property.

params.name: inspectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the inspection.

definition.description: Subcollection to store documents related to each property. Provides structured storage for property-related documents.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the property.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document.

definition.description: Subcollection to store expenses for each property.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the property.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the expense.

definition.description: Collection to store tenant information.  Stores details about tenants renting properties managed within the application.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The unique identifier for the tenant.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to manage rental properties, tenants, maintenance logs, inspections, and documents. The top-level collections are `properties`, `tenants` and `users/{userId}`. The subcollections `maintenanceLogs`, `inspections`, and `documents` are nested under each `property` to ensure data locality and facilitate querying related information. This structure leverages path-based ownership for user-specific data (e.g., properties owned by a user), which simplifies security rules. The design prioritizes Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) by avoiding hierarchical authorization dependencies (`get()`).  All collections are designed to support QAPs, as listing operations will be secured by path-based rules and potentially by filtering on denormalized fields, ensuring that users can only access data they are authorized to view.
